```json
  "findings": [
    "DTI (Direct Tension Indicator) squirter washers are not fully crushed, indicating bolts have not been tensioned to the required preload.",
    "Incomplete squiring of DTI protrusions violates RCSC Specification for fully tensioned high-strength bolts.",
    "Bolted connection may not develop the required clamping force and slip resistance per design."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Verify bolt grade, size, and DTI type against approved submittals and RCSC requirements.",
    "Retighten or replace bolts and DTIs as needed until all protrusions are fully compressed and visible gaps are eliminated per RCSC criteria.",
    "Perform inspection using a feeler gauge or equivalent method to confirm proper DTI compression.",
    "Document corrective action and obtain third-party inspection sign-off before closing out the work."
  ],
  "rationale": "RCSC Specification requires DTI squirts to be fully crushed when the bolt is properly tensioned. Partially crushed DTIs mean the connection has not achieved the specified preload, creating a structural deficiency that must be corrected before acceptance.",
  "references": [
    "RCSC Specification for Structural Joints Using High-Strength Bolts"
  ]
}
```
